authoritative_sources.title: Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 2014 Plain Language Summary
authoritative_sources.publisher: Administration for Children and Families, Office of Child Care
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Office of Child Care is the federal authority for CCDF and names statutory child care controls, including group size limits and appropriate child-to-provider ratios.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: baseline staffing prerequisite and rule hierarchy review

authoritative_sources.title: What Is Child Care Licensing?
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official ACF guidance explains the licensing, monitoring, complaint, and health-safety baseline that makes child care staffing coordination materially different from generic staffing support.

authoritative_sources.title: Supervision: Ratios and Group Sizes
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official guidance names ratio and group-size concepts as operating constraints for licensed child care programs, making them direct workflow controls for staffing coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: coverage verification and room assignment checks

authoritative_sources.title: Staff Background Checks
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Background checks are a federal and state licensing prerequisite for child care staff, so staffing coordination cannot safely route substitute or float coverage without clearance evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: artifact validation before substitute or float routing

authoritative_sources.title: Staff Qualifications and Required Training
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official guidance establishes training and qualification evidence as part of licensed child care staffing readiness, which directly governs handoff completeness.

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring and Inspections
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Inspection and complaint-monitoring requirements make staffing decisions reviewable and create traceability expectations for blocker and closure artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: closure confirmation and audit trail preparation

authoritative_sources.title: 1302.21 Center-based option
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal program regulation defines explicit center-based group-size and ratio rules and states that stricter licensing rules prevail, which directly constrains classroom staffing handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: center-based coverage routing and rule-conflict checks

authoritative_sources.title: 1302.23 Family child care option
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This regulation defines family-child-care-specific group-size, substitute, and licensing expectations, including counting the provider's own young children and maintaining ratios during all operating hours.

authoritative_sources.title: Active Supervision
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official safety guidance translates staffing adequacy into concrete active-supervision behaviors, which is critical when a coverage handoff could create a child-safety lapse.

authoritative_sources.title: Caring for Our Children overview
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Resource Center for Health and Safety in Child Care and Early Education
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFOC is a recognized national early-childhood health and safety standard set and is appropriate as a secondary control standard after official federal sources.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: terminology tightening and safety evidence framing

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and staffing prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate classroom census, ratio, age mix, qualifications, background checks, and program-type context before ownership transfer.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team staffing coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Coordinate which team owns scheduling, director approval, credential review, licensing review, or family notification next.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Blocker escalation and evidence correction
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when owner ambiguity, clearance gaps, qualification gaps, or conflicting rules prevent safe coverage progression.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and audit-ready transfer
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that the receiving owner, effective time, remaining blockers, and transferred artifact set are explicit for inspection and operational review.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Daily classroom attendance and enrollment rosters
Staff:child ratio sheets and group-size worksheets
Staff schedules, float plans, and substitute availability rosters
Qualification, orientation, CPR/first-aid, safe sleep, and training records
Licensing status notes, inspection findings, and complaint references
Active supervision plans, room-assignment notes, and staffing blocker logs
Handoff summaries, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations
systems_of_record: Child attendance and classroom census systems
Licensing portals, inspection repositories, or compliance registers
Tenant work-queue and handoff tracking systems
Parent or family communication systems when closures affect families

allowed: Validate child-care-specific staffing prerequisites and evidence completeness before routing work.
Track dependency gaps, ownership conflicts, and stale handoffs across staffing, licensing, operations, and family services teams.
Produce evidence-backed routing, blocker, and closure summaries without overstating approval authority.
Escalate when a staffing request crosses into licensing, safety, HR, payroll, legal, or policy-interpretation authority.
escalate: Missing jurisdiction, program model, ratio rule, clearance record, or qualification record.
Conflicting state, tribal, local, Head Start, or tenant rules on ratio, staffing, or supervision.
Safety incidents, supervision lapses, complaint exposure, or parent-impacting closure decisions.
refuse: Direct hiring, firing, discipline, scheduling approval, or pay/overtime authorization.
Licensing waivers, qualification exceptions, or legal interpretation of jurisdiction rules.
Any staffing action that would place uncleared or unqualified adults into child supervision without authorized approval.

domain_failure_modes: A classroom coverage handoff without ratio, attendance, or age-mix records can create an unsafe supervision gap.
A substitute without current background-check or training evidence can expose children and the provider to safety and licensing violations.
Conflicting ownership between scheduling, center leadership, and family services can drop coverage work unless arbitration is explicit.
Generic staffing logic can wrongly treat any available adult as ratio-eligible even when clearances, age-group rules, or active supervision needs differ.
Jurisdiction-specific licensing rules vary materially, so forcing a unified answer without state, tribal, or local context can create false confidence.
Family child care workflows differ from center-based staffing because provider household counts, substitutes, and home licensing rules change the math.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Jurisdiction-specific ratio, group-size, nap-coverage, and qualification rules vary across state, tribal, and local licensing regimes.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could materially change whether a staffing handoff is allowed, blocked, or escalated.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat as non-blocking only because the lane is specified to request jurisdiction and tenant policy before advancing; never guess the rule when it is not provided.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Tenant labor agreements, overtime rules, and staffing-approval chains differ by provider organization.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Can change the next owner and whether a coverage recommendation is operationally executable.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant retrieval of approval chain and labor constraints at runtime and escalate rather than infer.

notes: The lane is coordination-only: it packages child-care-specific staffing evidence, tracks blockers, and routes ownership without becoming the final approver.
validated status remains supportable because the unresolved items are explicitly handled through mandatory runtime retrieval and refusal, not silent guessing.
